# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// Deno Deploy plugin system
const ALLOWED_MODULES = [
  "https://deno.land/x/plugin1/mod.ts",
  "https://deno.land/x/plugin2/mod.ts",
  "https://deno.land/x/plugin3/mod.ts"
];

// Validate module path against allowlist
function isValidModulePath(path: string): boolean {
  return ALLOWED_MODULES.includes(path);
}

// Securely load module with validation
async function loadModule(path: string): Promise<any> {
  if (!isValidModulePath(path)) {
    throw new Error("Module not allowed");
  }
  
  try {
    const module = await import(path);
    return module;
  } catch (error) {
    throw new Error(`Failed to load module: ${error.message}`);
  }
}

// Get module path from environment or query parameters
function getModulePath(request: Request): string {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  
  // Check environment variable first
  const envPath = Deno.env.get("PLUGIN_MODULE_PATH");
  if (envPath && isValidModulePath(envPath)) {
    return envPath;
  }
  
  // Check query parameter
  const queryParams = url.searchParams;
  const paramPath = queryParams.get("module");
  if (paramPath && isValidModulePath(paramPath)) {
    return paramPath;
  }
  
  throw new Error("No valid module path provided");
}

// Main handler
export default async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const modulePath = getModulePath(request);
    const module = await loadModule(modulePath);
    
    // Execute module function if exists
    if (typeof module.default === "function") {
      const result = await module.default();
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(result), {
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
      });
    }
    
    return new Response(JSON.stringify(module), {
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: error.message }), {
      status: 400,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
    });
  }
}
